mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 6 months signals tightening monetary policy that raises the discount rate on future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuations compress.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20d forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns during rising-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in PAYEMS exceeding 200,000 net new jobs signals robust labor demand that boosts near-term consumer income expectations, triggering a short-lived positive S&P 500 reaction over the next 5 trading days as risk appetite increases.
failure_condition: In triggered months, 5d forward returns are positive in less than 55% of instances, or the mean triggered-period return is not statistically distinguishable from the unconditional 5d return distribution (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in HOUST and RSAFS reflects a broad-based expansion in household demand across housing and retail spending, jointly signaling durable consumption strength that supports higher equity earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter (60 trading days). Neither series alone captures this because housing starts can rise on supply-side incentives while retail sales can rise on transitory factors; only their joint acceleration indicates genuine demand-side strength.
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce 60d forward returns no better than episodes where only one series rises, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to Rank IC is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) versus the single-series baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO during an economic expansion signals improving industrial capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; this relationship is expected to be strongest during expansion phases and weak or reversed during late-cycle overheating when rising output triggers inflation and tightening fears.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns following INDPRO increases are not significantly positive (p > 0.05) during confirmed expansion regimes, or the sign of the relationship flips consistently across at least two separate expansion episodes.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE of 0.3 percentage points or more within a 3-month window signals a deteriorating labor market that raises recession probability and depresses forward-looking earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice risk.
failure_condition: Triggered UNRATE spike episodes show 5d forward returns positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean triggered return is not significantly different from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises faster than CPIAUCSL-implied inflation, the real policy rate increases sharply, tightening financial conditions beyond what nominal rates alone suggest; this joint condition produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days) because real borrowing costs for firms and consumers rise disproportionately. Neither FEDFUNDS nor CPIAUCSL alone captures this real-rate effect.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 6-month change minus CPIAUCSL annualized 6-month inflation change > 0.5pp
failure_condition: The real-rate interaction signal fails to outperform a single-series FEDFUNDS-only model in out-of-sample Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or 60d forward returns during triggered episodes are not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A steady positive trend in RSAFS over the trailing 3 months reflects resilient consumer spending, which sustains corporate revenue growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through an always-on demand-momentum channel.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS trend and 20d forward returns falls below 0.03 across any 3-year rolling window, or returns following positive RSAFS trends are not significantly higher than returns following flat/negative trends (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO growth decelerates below 1% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains elevated in a late-cycle expansion, the combination signals an impending supply-side slowdown compounded by restrictive policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days) as markets anticipate margin compression and reduced capital investment.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce 60d forward returns positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean triggered return is not significantly negative relative to unconditional baseline (p > 0.10).
